purpose: Implements EscapeHtml and EscapeScriptString. EscapeHtml does the standard 5-character HTML entity substitution. EscapeScriptString hardens UTF-16 text for safe inclusion inside a single-quoted JavaScript string literal embedded in a <script> tag (escapes \, ', \r, \n, line/paragraph separators, and unicode-escapes < > & so it cannot break out of the script).
invariants: Output is pure transformation of input; no external state.
gotchas: EscapeScriptString assumes the surrounding JS literal uses single quotes; double-quoted literals would not be properly escaped by it.
U+2028 / U+2029 are treated as line terminators by the JS spec - escaping them is mandatory, not cosmetic.
